description: SDK conformance corpus. Every entry below MUST produce the same verify outcome across the Rust, TypeScript, and Python SDKs — that's the conformance contract. CI runs each SDK's runner against this manifest; drift between SDKs (e.g., one accepts a tampered capsule the others reject) is the regression mode this catches.

vectors.name: v1_golden_001_allow_keeperhub_mock
vectors.comment: Canonical v1 allow path against KH mock — every SDK must accept.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_001_deny_with_execution_ref
vectors.comment: deny-decision capsule that nevertheless carries an execution_ref — violates the policy invariant. All SDKs must reject.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_002_mock_anchor_marked_live
vectors.comment: mock-anchor reference mislabelled as live — bidirectional invariant violation.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_003_live_mode_without_evidence
vectors.comment: live-mode capsule missing live_evidence — incomplete attestation.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_004_request_hash_mismatch
vectors.comment: request.request_hash != receipt.request_hash — primary integrity check fails.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_005_policy_hash_mismatch
vectors.comment: policy.policy_hash != receipt.policy_hash — same shape, policy side.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_006_malformed_checkpoint
vectors.comment: audit.checkpoint structurally malformed.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_007_unknown_field
vectors.comment: Unknown top-level field — Rust strict schema rejects; Python SDK currently accepts (gap: Py's structural verifier doesn't enforce additionalProperties:false at the v1 layer). Conformance runners SKIP this vector for SDKs in known_drift; backlog: harden Py SDK to match Rust.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_008_live_mode_empty_evidence
vectors.comment: live-mode with empty live_evidence object — minProperties violation.

vectors.name: v1_tampered_009_executor_evidence_empty_object
vectors.comment: executor_evidence empty object — Rust enforces minProperties:1, Python SDK currently accepts. Same backlog as #007.

vectors.name: v2_golden_001_minimal
vectors.comment: Smallest v2 self-contained capsule — every SDK must accept.

vectors.name: v2_golden_002_keeperhub_mock
vectors.comment: v2 capsule against KH mock — accepts.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_001_policy_snapshot_drift
vectors.comment: Structurally well-formed; embedded policy_snapshot doesn't hash to claimed policy_hash. Caught by STRICT (cryptographic) verify, passes structural verify.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_002_audit_segment_chain_break
vectors.comment: Structurally well-formed; audit_segment has prev_event_hash linkage break. Caught by strict-mode chain verify.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_003_audit_segment_link_mismatch
vectors.comment: Structurally well-formed; summary.audit_event_id ≠ capsule audit.audit_event_id. Caught by strict-mode audit_event_link check.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_004_audit_segment_too_large
vectors.comment: Structurally well-formed at the surface; audit_segment exceeds the 1MB envelope cap (DoS guard).

vectors.name: v2_tampered_005_executor_evidence_drift
vectors.comment: R20 Task C — execution.executor_evidence is a bare string; schema's `oneOf [null, object]` rejects, Rust verifier surfaces capsule.schema_invalid. Python/TS SDKs may not enforce the oneOf strictly today.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_006_reverse_chain_link
vectors.comment: R20 Task C — seq=1 audit event's prev_event_hash points at its own event_hash (self-cycle / forward edge). Structural pass; strict audit_chain catches the linkage break (PrevHashMismatch).

vectors.name: v2_tampered_007_signature_swap
vectors.comment: R20 Task C — receipt signature_hex is valid 128-hex-char shape but does NOT verify under the published receipt-signer pubkey. Caught by strict receipt_signature.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_008_replay_with_new_nonce
vectors.comment: R20 Task C — request.aprp.nonce regenerated post-receipt; the recomputed JCS+SHA-256 of the mutated APRP no longer matches the claimed request_hash. Caught by strict request_hash_recompute. Offline counterpart of the daemon's HTTP nonce-replay gate.

vectors.name: v2_tampered_009_oversized_audit_segment
vectors.comment: R20 Task C — 10,000-event audit_chain_segment (~8 MiB) exceeds the 1 MiB cap. Same gate as v2_tampered_004 but exercised via a real-shape long chain rather than a padding field.

notes: Every fixture path is relative to test-corpus/.
schema_version reflects the capsule's `schema` field — 1 for sbo3l.passport_capsule.v1, 2 for sbo3l.passport_capsule.v2.

verify_ok is the SDK's structural verify result. Strict (cryptographic) verify is exercised separately in each SDK's strict-mode tests; this manifest stays at the structural layer because that surface exists in all three SDKs today.

known_drift: optional array of SDK names where the current implementation disagrees with verify_ok. Runners SKIP the vector for SDKs in this list and emit a `pending` marker. The Rust SDK is treated as the canonical reference; entries here are backlog items to harden the listed SDK.

When adding a new fixture, write the .json file under test-corpus/passport/ AND append an entry here with the outcome the Rust verifier produces. CI catches drift if a future SDK change disagrees with the manifest.
